novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=letter below novelty_target=flagship: the coordinatewise stitched relaxation is introduced by the proposal, but no cited published paper, estimator, or workflow is quoted as actually using that relaxation.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The open-class strict-gap claim has only an edge-capacity contradiction; §9 does not specify a complete finite observable law P, outcome table, and support functional showing distinct joint versus stitched values.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The headline promises a sharp finite joint object and strict containment, but Exhibit 9.1 computes only hidden-edge infeasibility, not the actual Θ_joint/Θ_stitch bound or support value from §6 primitives.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship gap cites Sävje/Hoshino/Schröder at a high level, but lacks a location-level anchor for the specific finite-sharp-bound or stitched-relaxation gap.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Name the exact published workflow being refuted, preferably a bound or estimator in Hoshino-Yanagi, Schröder et al., or Nibbering-Oosterveen, and quote the section/equation that licenses the stitched-comparator target.
Replace Exhibit 9.1 with a complete finite experiment: full π_Z support, take-up tables, μ table, observed moments P, a linear functional v, and computed h(v;Θ_joint) < h(v;Θ_stitch).
Add location anchors in §4/§5 for each flagship comparator: section, equation, or quoted claim showing exactly what Hoshino-Yanagi and Schröder et al. leave unresolved.

recommended_next_step: Revise Conjecture 1 around a named published comparator and a fully computed finite witness; otherwise the upgrade remains below the flagship floor.

upgrade_review.upgrade_delta_one_line: The draft genuinely widens the parent by adding monotone noncompliance and a second misspecified exposure channel, with an explicit parent-corner reduction.
